title: Replace :latest with immutable tags or digests
summary: The :latest tag is a moving pointer; pin by digest or a dated/immutable tag for auditability.
body:
Registries resolve :latest to whatever image was most recently tagged, which can change silently. For production, reference an immutable tag (e.g. release semver) or @sha256:... so GitOps diffs and admission reviews refer to exact bytes.

CI should fail merges that introduce :latest in production overlays; dev clusters may relax with explicit documentation.

steps: Choose a release tag or digest from your artifact registry after build.
Replace image: repo/app:latest with repo/app:v1.2.3 or repo/app@sha256:...
Reconcile Argo CD / kubectl apply and verify rollouts reference the new reference.
remediation.example_manifest_snippet:
containers:
  - name: aegisml
    image: registry.example.com/aegisml:v1.2.3
    # preferred:
    # image: registry.example.com/aegisml@sha256:abcdef...
remediation.verification: grep -R ':latest' k8s/ and ensure production overlays have no matches.

nist.name: Developer Configuration Management
nist.discussion: Immutable references tie deployed software to approved builds.

nist.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
nist.discussion: Prevents undetected substitution of workload images.

fedramp.notes: Tie image changes to approved releases and change tickets.
